extracted_information: GitHub Codespaces provides cloud-based development environments, featuring security, collaboration tools, and flexible access options. It offers a free tier for individuals and pay-as-you-go pricing for organizations, with flexible compute options.

compute_options.machine_types: VM-based compute options hosted by GitHub.com
compute_options.core_configurations: From 2 core machines up to 32 core machines

individual_free_tier.core_hours: 120 core hours per month OR 60 hours of run time on a 2 core codespace per month
individual_free_tier.storage: 15 GB of storage per month included

pricing.organizational_pricing_model: Pay-as-you-go pricing with customizable spending limits
pricing.general_pricing_model: Simple pay-as-you-go pricing applies after individual free tier
pricing.student_access: Free as part of GitHub Student Developer Pack
pricing.open_source_access: Generous free monthly usage for maintainers and contributors
pricing.storage_pricing_details: Specific per-GB pricing for storage beyond the free tier is not detailed in this content, but is part of the general pay-as-you-go model.

features: Fully configured, secure cloud development environments native to GitHub
Secure by design: Isolated Environments, Access Control, Cost Control
Shared development environment, removes complex and time-consuming setups
Customizable: use dotfiles for personalized settings, feels like a local machine
Instant coding from anywhere, preconfigured for projects, settings travel with you
Browser preview and port forwarding (share ports within policy scope)
Faster onboarding: quick spin-up with only an IDE or browser and a GitHub account; instant, fully configured, secure dev environment for developers
Code from any device (e.g., iPad), lives in the cloud (no powerful local device needed)
Launches instantly from any repository with pre-configured environments
Fix bugs directly from a pull request (open Codespaces from PR without waiting for dev environment load)
Integration with LinkedIn Learning for hands-on practice
Access from browser or locally using IDE (Visual Studio Code, IntelliJ)
enterprise_features: Available for teams and companies (needs to be enabled in organization's settings)
Organizations can select which repositories and users have access for added security and permissioning control
Organizations need to pay for, enable, and manage their Codespaces instances
Pricing controls for organizations to manage monthly spend
Organization policies can prevent cloning (access control at organization level)
